title: anil tinctoria var. normalis
intro: Anil tinctoria var. normalis, also known as the Common Madder, is a perennial plant belonging to the madder family and is native to parts of Asia and Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits associated with Anil tinctoria var. normalis include reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and digestive issues. Anil tinctoria var. normalis is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal medicine.
